rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAMBERT GENZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural Issues on Steven Kmucha's Petition: 1) It only lists that the decedent was survived by two of other next of kin. Pursuant to LRSF 14.18.C.3) , Petition needs to provide detail ancestors info so that the court can review the notice requirement under PC sec. 8110(b). Declaration filed 2/24/2023; however, it presents paternal family tree only, and the tree is not complete, either. Procedural Issues on Qubao Yu's Petition: 1) Pursuant to LRSF 14.18.C. 3, Petition needs to provide detail ancestors (of Petitioner and Ms. West) info so that the court can review the notice requirement under PC sec. 8110(b). 2) Notice to Public Administrator 3) Proposed Order to be submitted. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN R. HANSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/2023. A verified supplemental by the fiduciary is needed to provide the following information: (1) Allegation re notice of estate administration to Franchise Tax Board. PC 9202(c). Paragraph#9 states notice to DHCS only. (2) Update if the distribution from the estate of Ruth H. Stewart has been made to this estate or not. Petition and I&A describe said distribution is anticipated; however, appears if the funds are not in the executor's control, this petition appears premature. Please supplement the information seven days prior to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TEDMAN MARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/17/2023, in light of counsel's declaration filed on 03/16/2023. Nothing has been filed to address the following issues (tentative ruling for 02/28/2023 hearing): 1) The information required by LRSF 14.43(D)(2)-(4) must be provided. 2) The petition must be presented under Probate Code section 850 to request an order compelling "all non-party holders of property" to transfer the funds to the estate, and each non-party property holder must be served in the manner required by section 851(a)(2). See also section 856. 3) The petition requests the Court's retention of jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement under CCP section 664.6, but the underlying 850 petition was taken off calendar at the 10/26/2022 hearing without objections and is no longer pending. Do the parties to the settlement agreement qualify as "parties to pending litigation" within the meaning of section 664.6(a)? The settlement agreement does not contain a provision requiring any party to request the Court's retention of jurisdiction, nor was the Court presented with the request to retain jurisdiction before the 850 petition was taken off calendar. See Hagan Engineering, Inc. v. Mills (2003) 115 Cal.App.4th 1004; see also Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960. 4) The settlement agreement contains a confidentiality clause, but an unredacted copy of the agreement has already been filed and is publicly viewable.
